prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark, low-visibility shot. Most of the frame is black or shadowed. On the right there is a reddish-brown vertical surface, possibly a wall or doorway, and near the center there is a dim greenish vertical area that may be part of a door or interior structure. No people, products, or readable text are visible in this opening frame.

By the final frame, the image has changed to a bright product-display shot. A hand and forearm enter from the right, holding a yellow aerosol can upright. The can appears to be a Glade product. The background resembles a domestic kitchen or household setting, with wooden cabinet doors across the upper portion of the frame and a pale surface below. Near the lower center, bold black on-screen text reads: “Newest of the Natural Scents from Glade.” To the left of the text there is a small rectangular picture or label showing a natural-looking image, though the details are not fully clear. The final frame functions as a clear product and slogan-style visual end card.
